title: Dennis Quaid's Audience-Approved Presidential Epic Gets Historical Re-Release

blocks.content: Dennis Quaid has starred in two major biopics throughout his career, his most recent arguably being his most divisive. While critics gave it a dismal 17%, audiences overwhelmingly embraced this 2024 film with a Rotten Tomatoes score of 98%. The film was also nominated for six Razzies, including Worst Picture and Worst Actor (for Quaid). Regardless of the mixed reception, Reagan is getting a theatrical re-release next month, and it promises to be historical.

Reagan will be screened for a limited time in more than 12 cities over Fourth of July weekend, followed by a wider re-release to about 600 theaters in September. The film will have 10 minutes of unreleased footage involving Ronald Reagan (Quaid) and his wife Nancy (Penelope Ann Miller), Reagan with his alcoholic father Jack (Justin Chatwin), and scenes at Rancho del Cielo, the late president's California ranch. This extended cut bumps the film's runtime to about 2.5 hours.

“For some reason, they thought I would be the guy,” Quaid shared exclusively with MovieWeb in the build up to the movie's initial release ijn 2024. “And I didn't think I was the guy. One thing, Reagan was my favorite president. Everybody knows the public Reagan. So, it took me a while. I went to the Reagan Ranch, and going there, I could feel him as a human being; kind of his core was there at the Reagan Ranch. And I realized Reagan was a humble person. He was not a rich guy. I think a lot of people think that he was. I think a lot of people got rich during his administration. But he was a humble man.”

Reagan does take its sweet time in unraveling its plot. The opening scene might also irk critics for suggesting the late president's pivotal role in the fall of the Soviet Union. After touching on Reagan's early start as a lifeguard and radio announcer, the film ramps up, depicting the rise and decline of his acting career, and how he became an FBI informant. The rest of the film focuses on Reagan's political aspirations and his precarious two terms as president.
Dennis Quaid Has a Knack for Playing U.S. Presidents

While Dennis Quaid had some trepidation over played Ronald Reagan, it's far from the first time the actor has played a POTUS. Quaid also played President Bill Clinton in 2010's The Special Relationship, while his brother, Randy Quaid, impersonated Ronald Reagan seven times on SNL. Despite his initial worries, Quaid clearly did a great job, with Reagan reportedly being embraced by the former president's closest allies and aides, as well as audiences.

As for Quaid's other biopic, that was 1989's Great Balls of Fire!, in which he played rockabilly pioneer Jerry Lee Lewis. Reception for that film was mildly mixed, garnering 53% and 65% scores from audiences and critics. Both Great Balls of Fire! and Reagan flopped at the box office, the latter grossing $30 million against its $25 million budget. Reagan re-releases in select US theaters on Jul. 4.
